item.title: Why steel tariffs are rumbling the EU’s outreach to South Korea
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Brussels looks likely to cheer Italian bank merger

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: FirstFT: US and Iran trade fire after downing of American helicopter
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: alternative to Patriots and US equity supplies

item.title: Apollo and Blackstone’s Large Lending Model
item.description: The private capital groups have finalised a complex $35bn deal that will help finance Anthropic’s growth plans

item.title: Mercedes set to partner with drone defence start-up Tytan
item.description: German carmaker to provide vehicles for interception of threats that pose a growing concern for Europe

item.title: Why the world must agree to regulate AI
item.description: A technological disarmament pact between the US and China would make everyone feel more secure

item.title: Ukraine builds cheap alternative to Patriot missiles
item.description: Arms maker Fire Point says it has successfully tested surface-to-air projectiles

item.title: ECB moved to rein in Revolut’s ‘self-guided missiles’ in Europe
item.description: Europe’s most valuable fintech was ordered to address ‘deficiencies’ in oversight with staff encouraged to rapidly launch new financial products

item.title: Homeowners are getting used to the UK’s perma-crisis
item.description: Households appear to have adapted to a constant state of flux

item.title: Wall Street’s IPO boom threatens era of shrinking US stock supply
item.description: Market debuts of SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI plus slowing buybacks could remove vital support for equities, investors warn

item.title: Thames Water to pay £749mn under creditor takeover deal
item.description: Costs mount for restructuring of UK’s biggest water company as it strives to avoid nationalisation

item.title: ‘You can’t fly planes that are empty’: Airlines draw up cuts for ‘ugly’ winter
item.description: Fears of stubbornly high jet fuel prices overshadow annual industry gathering

item.title: Top Goldman executive opposed David Solomon’s backing of Kathy Ruemmler
item.description: Russell Horwitz, one of few executives to raise the taboo subject, is leaving the bank

item.title: Germany’s €100bn bid to make the trains run on time
item.description: The country’s railway renewal is the first test of whether Europe’s largest economy can reverse years of decline

item.title: Warburg Pincus and KKR tap buyers for UK broadband businesses
item.description: Altnet sector grapples with lower-than-expected customer fibre uptake and higher costs

item.title: How SpaceX’s IPO ambitions ride on Starship’s path to Mars
item.description: Musk’s largest rocket must prove it can routinely fly before the company can deliver on its biggest promises

item.title: The pension system missed Gen X women — what if they are the future?
item.description: A sandwich generation caught between two very different savings systems

item.title: DR Congo’s curbs on cobalt spark squeeze in vital battery element
item.description: A shortage of the raw material has sent Chinese smelters scrambling to find alternatives

item.title: China crackdown brings drone users down to earth
item.description: Tough new rules shake companies in world’s biggest producer of uncrewed aerial vehicles

item.title: Chinese companies rush to dismantle ‘red-chip’ structures
item.description: Beijing’s tighter scrutiny of foreign capital forces groups to rethink ownership structures behind listings boom

item.title: Chinese investors turn to digital bets for exposure to US tech IPOs
item.description: Manoeuvres highlight the lengths traders are willing to go to get exposure to hot western investments despite risks

item.title: When Nixon put America first and took the dollar off gold
item.description: US to the world: 'It’s our dollar, but it’s your problem'

item.title: Australia tries to fix its housing crisis. Will it work?
item.description: Anthony Albanese is aiming to undo decades of tax incentives and make property more affordable for younger buyers

item.title: Apollo and Blackstone raise $35bn in chip financing deal for Anthropic
item.description: Transaction is one of the largest private credit fundraisings, fuelling Claude maker’s AI growth plans

item.title: Apollo hunts for Japanese life insurer to boost growth
item.description: US asset manager may face regulatory hurdles amid preference for domestic players

item.title: GM bets on homegrown battery tech to challenge Chinese dominance
item.description: US carmakers pivot to energy storage batteries for AI as they seek to counter weak electric vehicle sales

item.title: Why are investors so jumpy?
item.description: What’s driving market volatility?

item.title: Oil slides as US official says Hormuz transits are ‘meaningfully’ climbing
item.description: Energy secretary Chris Wright cautions that flows through the strait will take months to fully recover

item.title: Nasdaq sinks more than 3% as tech sell-off reignites
item.description: Heavy losses for AI-linked stocks overshadow gains across rest of market

item.title: GSK’s $10.6bn cancer deal will lead to more
item.description: Making additional moves would be in keeping with pharma group’s strategy of building its pipeline as fast as possible
